body:
Bead ddx-921616ea is the Story 13 epic. Its sole acceptance criterion is
"All child beads closed." Six children carry the testable ACs:

  - ddx-41481396  B1a: FEAT-005/TD-005 amendments + library/artifacts/met/ bundle
  - ddx-88507ed5  B1b: author MET-001 (exec) and MET-002 (external) artifacts
  - ddx-d75ae69a  B2:  ddx doc validate enforces metric_id; budget/ratchet warning
  - ddx-70467059  B3:  ddx metric list/show + metric.go:32 fix + README cleanup
  - ddx-296019fe  B4:  integration round-trip + mixed-unit refusal
  - ddx-cd42fc05  B5:  FEAT-014/FEAT-016 cross-reference edits

All six are currently open (verified via `ddx bead list --where
parent=ddx-921616ea`). Until they close, the epic's AC cannot be
satisfied — there is no implementation work owned by the epic itself.

What is done: nothing in this attempt; epic is purely a roll-up.

What is blocking: completion of the six children listed above. They
should be drained in dependency order (B1a → B1b/B2 → B3 → B4; B5
parallel to B1a).

What a follow-up attempt would need: re-queue this epic only after all
six children are closed. At that point closing the epic is a one-line
status flip with no code change.
